t_ch: 赋予英雄们姓名的利刃，无畏狂妄，战无不胜。
t_en: A fearless and reckless blade that won every battle for its wielder, and bestowed its name upon many heroes.

t_ch: 可如今，你的疯狂折磨着凡人的灵魂，这悬于众生头顶的刀锋，
t_en: But now your insanity tortures mortals' souls. O, this blade that hangs about the heads of humanity,

t_ch: 落下吧！让悲戚的冤魂重新回到大地。
t_en: Fall! Let the somber and wronged spirits return to the land once again.

t_ch: 征伐行道
t_en: Path of Conquest

t_ch: 旧日的荣光，悬锋的王，荣耀是你的名。
t_en: Glory of the olden days, King of Kremnos, honor be your name.

t_ch: 向你祈祷，尼卡多利，请用纷争赐我以丰盛的和平！
t_en: I pray to you, Nikador. Please grant me abundant peace through strife!

t_ch: 琢石区
t_en: Chisel Zone

t_ch: 「歌耳戈之子，告诉我，你心底藏着怎样的梦？只要一句愿望，我将为你展开奇迹。」
t_en: "Child of Gorgo, tell me, what dreams lie at the bottom of your heart? One wish, and I shall perform a miracle for you."

t_ch: 「我想…去岁月里流浪，或成为天空的一束光。」
t_en: "I want to... wander through the years, or become a beam of light in the sky."

t_ch: 「哎，让我们再来一遍…歌耳戈之子，告诉我，是军锋，铁卫，还是射手？」
t_en: "Let us try... one more time, child of Gorgo. A vanguard, an aegis, or a marksman?"

t_ch: 「永恒之城」奥赫玛
t_en: Okhema, the Eternal City

t_ch: 或背负黄昏，或背负黎明。
t_en: It bears the dusk on its back, and it bears the dawn on its back.

t_ch: 它们在岁月的长河中相融又分离。
t_en: They unite and separate in the river of time.

t_ch: 然后迎来崭新的昨日。
t_en: And then they welcome a brand-new yesterday.

t_ch: 掘石场
t_en: Excavation Ground

t_ch: 分崩的靛石溅起粉尘，混杂熔岩滚过的烟气。
t_en: The air is filled with dust from the shattered indigo stones, and mixed with the smoke from the lava.

t_ch: 自动化的采石器屈从铁一般的纪律，不知昼夜。
t_en: The automatic excavators work with extreme discipline and know nothing of time.

t_ch: 结束劳作的工人登上层旋的石阶，爬出矿井，在喟叹间熄灭火把的照明。
t_en: At the end of their shift, workers ascend the spiral staircase to exit the shaft and extinguish their torches as they sigh.

t_ch: 抬头已是黎明。
t_en: When they look up, dawn has already arrived.

t_ch: 铸魂区
t_en: Soul-Forging Zone

t_ch: 他妒忌，他图谋，他执迷不悟。
t_en: He was jealous, cunning, and foolishly stubborn.

t_ch: 他错过了最后的机会，塞纳托斯的迷魇罩住了他，使他丧失理智。
t_en: Having missed his last chance, the nightmares of Thanatos enveloped him and drove away his sanity.

t_ch: 从此他看见天上有两个太阳。
t_en: From this day, he saw two suns in the sky.

t_ch: 永恒的事，只有不死的众神才能索求。
t_en: Only the immortal gods can seek what is eternal.

t_ch: 靛石传送区
t_en: Indigo Steel teleportation zone

t_ch: 错综的地形，是最好的训练场。
t_en: Areas with intricate terrain make the best training grounds.

t_ch: 年幼的王储在高悬的铁链间穿行，步履轻盈。
t_en: The young crown prince walks lightly on his feet, traversing the iron shackles above.

t_ch: 身后的下人们气喘吁吁，生怕踩中了落雷。
t_en: His servants huff and puff behind him, terrified to step upon a bolt of lightning.

t_ch: 圣树
t_en: Sacred Tree

t_ch: 巨人的梦语垂入大地，长成参天的我。
t_en: The dreams of giants sank into the earth and grew into towering colossus like me.

t_ch: 知识像乘着风的种子，令百花盛开大地，枯枝也绽出新芽——
t_en: Knowledge, like seeds borne on the wind, causes flowers to bloom across the land, and withered branches to sprout anew —

t_ch: 世界向我倾泻而来，如山泉；我双目清明，却看不清自己。
t_en: The world pours itself upon me like a spring. My eyes are clear, yet I cannot see myself.

t_ch: 友爱之馆
t_en: Library of Philia

t_ch: 致求知的灵魂——
t_en: To the soul that seeks understanding —

t_ch: 爱与智慧，本是同一个生命；
t_en: Love and wisdom are but one life.

t_ch: 口渴的人来到水边，却融化在月亮的倒影中。
t_en: The thirsty approach the water, only to dissolve in the moon's reflection.

t_ch: 献身拱心
t_en: Dome of Devotion

t_ch: 爱对我说——
t_en: Love spoke to me —

t_ch: 「我是不羁的风，飘然而至，与你的火焰相拥。
t_en: "I am the untamed wind, arriving unbidden to embrace your flame.

t_ch: 温柔地缠绕，令你火光熊熊，而后缓缓熄灭。」
t_en: Gently entwining, I stoke your fire to brilliance, then let it slowly die down."

t_ch: 慈爱之庭
t_en: Courtyard of Storge

t_ch: 回忆中，那是场金色的雨——
t_en: In memory, it was a golden rain —

t_ch: 厄洛缇亚满载世界的爱，乘着黎明归来，
t_en: Eroteia, full of the world's love, returned at dawn,

t_ch: 化作蝴蝶散下金光闪闪的鳞粉，令落及的生灵血脉相融，
t_en: Transforming into butterflies that scattered glittering scale dust, blending the bloodlines of those they touched.

t_ch: 从此往后，你中有我，我中有你。
t_en: From that day forward, there was you in me, and I in you.

t_ch: 神殿入口
t_en: Temple Entrance

t_ch: 是的，它们旧梦未泯——
t_en: Yes, their old dreams have not faded —

t_ch: 尘封千年的门扉再启，内殿的烛光亮如白昼。
t_en: The doors sealed for a millennium reopen, and the candles within burn bright as daylight.

t_ch: 朝拜者走入命运深处，接受温暖如初的怀抱，仿佛一切从未远离。
t_en: Pilgrims step into the depths of destiny, welcomed by an embrace as warm as ever, as if nothing had ever been lost.

t_ch: 预言书库
t_en: Archive of Prophecies

t_ch: 殿内卷宗堆叠如经纬，命运的丝线勾连过去与未来。
t_en: Within the hall, scrolls are stacked like warp and weft, threads of destiny weaving past and future.

t_ch: 书桌上笔墨未干，烛光照亮未解的神谕。
t_en: The ink on the desk remains wet, and candlelight illuminates the unresolved prophecy.

t_ch: 「全世之座」刻法勒
t_en: "Throne of Worlds" Kephale

t_ch: 它的话语穿过长夜，敲我的门——
t_en: Their voice pierces the long night, knocking at my door —

t_ch: 「解开你受缚的翅膀！你将粉身碎骨，希望也在此刻发生；
t_en: "Unbind your wings! Though you will shatter, hope is born in this moment.

t_ch: 看，百眼的巨鸟就要苏醒，
t_en: Behold, the hundred-eyed bird stirs to awaken.

t_ch: 莫错过雅努萨波利斯最后的日出。」
t_en: Do not miss the last sunrise of Janusopolis."

t_ch: 雅努斯宝库
t_en: Janus Vault

t_ch: 雅努斯的火种存放于此，
t_en: The Coreflame of Janus is housed here.

t_ch: 任信徒千呼万唤，空有回音，
t_en: Though countless believers call for them, humanity's voices echo unanswered.

t_ch: 静默伫候命定的时刻。
t_en: Silent and unmoving, awaiting their destined hour.

t_ch: 火山为座的巨湖
t_en: The Great Lake Cradled by a Volcano

t_ch: 负世的天父在此泼洒金血，注入凡世的湖。
t_en: Here the Worldbearing sky father spilled their golden blood into the mortal lake.

t_ch: 金血随涧泉下降，黎明随云雾上升。
t_en: The golden blood flows down with spring waters, as dawn rises with the mists.

t_ch: 庄严的寂静中再无一语，火山的心脏自此也再未跳动。
t_en: No words ever break the solemn silence, and the volcano's heart beats no more.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的公民议会
t_en: The Citizens' Assembly of Okhema

t_ch: 「噤声，公民，你要知道自己正步入何地。」
t_en: "Quiet, citizen, know where you stand."

t_ch: ——公民议会，万众瞩望的风向标，真理锤凿的淬炼场。
t_en: — The Citizens' Assembly, the beacon of public opinion, a crucible where truth is forged.

t_ch: 瀑流会涤去谵妄的渣滓，而理性将经由论辩的回火愈加显彰。
t_en: The falls cleanse away delirious dregs, while reason sharpens through the tempering of debates.

t_ch: 诸神注目的剧场
t_en: The Theater Beneath the Gods' Watch

t_ch: 凡人辩诘的议会，诸神注目的剧场。
t_en: An assembly for mortal debates, a theater under the gods' watch.

t_ch: 在这里——一切造物的中央，我们竟胆敢倾听、言说、探讨，
t_en: Here, at the center of all beings, we dare to listen, speak, and deliberate,

t_ch: 推演规则、制定律法、宣判死亡。
t_en: Reason out rules, establish laws, and pronounce death.

t_ch: 神明啊——你们是否将此付之一笑？
t_en: Oh gods — does this amuse you?

t_ch: 泰坦断崖
t_en: The Titan Cliff

t_ch: 坍圮的石阶，朝拜者拓印下足迹和期冀。
t_en: Upon the crumbling stairs, pilgrims imprint their steps and aspirations.

t_ch: 又一次登上九十九级长阶，扎格列斯老祖也难免汗如雨下——
t_en: Climbing these ninety-nine steps, even ancestor Zagreus was soaked in sweat—

t_ch: 「泰坦徒劳践行宿命，信徒蹒跚亦步亦趋。」
t_en: "Titans trudge on, following fate, while believers stumble after their steps."

t_ch: 刻法勒的俯身
t_en: Kephale's Lean

t_ch: 虔诚的使徒、渎神的学者，
t_en: Devoted emissaries, heretical scholars,

t_ch: 失却故乡的羁旅者和穷途末路的亡命徒。
t_en: The lost wanderer, and the desperate outlaw.

t_ch: 在离神明最近的地方我们仰头凝望，
t_en: At the edge of divinity, we look up in awe.

t_ch: 在一瞥的震悚里遗忘身后长路。
t_en: In a fleeting moment of terror, the long road behind fades from memory.

t_ch: 泛滥的冥河
t_en: Overflowing River of Souls

t_ch: 冥河的引渡者啊——你究竟所在何方？
t_en: O Ferryman of the River of Souls — where are you?

t_ch: 虫豸正吞吃我的手指，荒草已覆盖我的背脊，可铎音仍无法唤回你，
t_en: Insects devour my fingers, wild grass creeps up my back, yet the bell's chimes cannot summon you,

t_ch: 正如它也早已在我腐败的耳中沉寂。
t_en: As they have long since fallen silent in my decaying ears.

t_ch: 昭示衰亡的月相
t_en: The Moon Phases of Decline

t_ch: 可曾有人遥望交错的月相，试图得见欧洛尼斯预见的未来？
t_en: Has anyone ever looked upon the intertwining moon phases, trying to glimpse the future foretold by Oronyx?

t_ch: 「双月是太阳的碎镜，在其中我们端详自己的面影。」
t_en: "The twin moons are broken mirrors of the sun. Within them, we see only reflections of ourselves."

t_ch: 或许艾格勒早已看透了一切的荒诞。
t_en: Perhaps Aquila has already seen through the absurdity of it all.

t_ch: 死龙的骸骨
t_en: The Bones of Netherwing

t_ch: 早夭的玻吕刻斯啊，
t_en: O Pollux, who died young,

t_ch: 你本应展开挂满白骨的双翼，向城邦吹送死亡的腥风，
t_en: You were meant to unfurl your wings of bones, and send the stench of death to the city-states,

t_ch: 如今却在安详的沉眠里，骨骸如鲸鱼般生满苔花。
t_en: Yet now you rest in peaceful slumber, your bones covered in moss, like a forgotten whale.

t_ch: 古城遗迹
t_en: The Ruins of the Ancient City

t_ch: 宴饮早已散场，酣歌也已停歇。
t_en: The feast has long ended, the songs long silenced.

t_ch: 晚钟敲响，
t_en: The evening bell tolls,

t_ch: 溺亡者沉浮的水面，仍将漫天星斗倒映。
t_en: And the drowned still drift, their faces mirrored in the stars above.

t_ch: 枯败的花园
t_en: The Withered Garden

t_ch: 所有火光都会熄灭，所有花朵都会凋落。
t_en: All flames will eventually die, all flowers will wilt.

t_ch: 枯败的花园里，踱步的旅人在一次呼吸间怆然老去，
t_en: In the withered garden, the traveler's steps grow slower, each breath aging them in sorrow,

t_ch: 月光移转，空影徘徊。
t_en: As moonlight shifts and shadows linger.

t_ch: 白厄，新的一批难民都已接引入城了么？
t_en: Phainon, has the latest batch of refugees been admitted into the city?

t_ch: 托缇宝老师的福，难民们都已安顿妥当了。希望圣城能治愈他们颠沛疲惫的身心。
t_en: Thanks to Lady Tribbie, the refugees have already settled. I hope that the holy city can heal their fatigued minds and bodies.

t_ch: 那么，这次又是哪个城邦？
t_en: Which city was it this time?

t_ch: …埃杜利亚，信仰瑟希斯的书之城……疯溃的泰坦眷属几乎把那个城邦翻了个底朝天，藏书也全都烧尽了。
t_en: ...Idilia. The city of books that worships Cerces... Those insane Titankin almost turned the city upside-down. All of their books were burned, too.

t_ch: 看来，回收尼卡多利火种一事已迫在眉睫了。你们需尽早决断，由谁来承载「天谴之矛」的火种。
t_en: It seems that the matter of reclaiming the Coreflame of Nikador is urgent. You must reach a verdict on who will bear the Coreflame of the Lance of Fury.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅，你理应知道，我只想亲手熄灭我族神明早已污浊的神魂。至于那颗火种，你们随意安排便是。
t_en: Aglaea, you must understand that all I want is to extinguish the long tainted soul of my clan's gods with my own two hands. As for the Coreflame, make arrangements as you see fitting.

t_ch: 你可真是自说自话啊，万敌，战士之间不需要谦让。等到了尼卡多利面前，我们应当凭实力夺取火种。
t_en: I see your concern only stretches as far as yourself, Mydei. There is no need for humility among warriors. When we are faced with Nikador, we should use our strength to seize the Coreflame.

t_ch: 还是说你怕了？觉得正当竞争比不过我，只好预先告饶？
t_en: Or are you saying that you are scared? Perhaps you think that your skill in battle is no match for mine, and you have no choice but to ask for mercy in advance?

t_ch: 可笑，悬锋人的字典里没有害怕二字。至于实力，你可以现在就试试……
t_en: Don't make me laugh. The Kremnoan dictionary does not contain the word "scared." As for a battle of strength, you are welcome to try me right now...

t_ch: 别吵啦！别吵啦！悬锋城现在飘到了哪里我们都还不知道呢，上哪儿找尼卡多利呀……
t_en: Stop fighting! Stop fighting! We don't know where Castrum Kremnos has drifted off to. Where are we supposed to go in search of Nikador...?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人，除却迫在眉睫的尼卡多利危机，还有一事。又有一座遥远的城邦失去联络了。
t_en: Lady Aglaea, we have another matter besides the looming threat of Nikador. We have lost contact with another distant city.

t_ch: 蝶，你的意思…那不是尼卡多利的手笔么？
t_en: Cas, are you saying... That it was not the work of Nikador?

t_ch: 尼卡多利再如何凶恶，也不可能刹那间倾覆整座城池，连一位幸存者都见不到。
t_en: No matter how ruthless Nikador is, there's no way they could raze an entire city in an instant without leaving a single survivor.

t_ch: …我明白了。我会遣赛飞儿去看看，如果她愿意的话。现在，我们就在奥赫玛静观其变吧。
t_en: ...I understand. I will send Cipher to take a look, if she is willing. For now, we shall have to sit and watch for any developments from inside Okhema.

t_ch: 金线日渐紧绷，却又偶尔传来些许生意盎然的波澜——恐有大事将要发生了。
t_en: The golden thread grows tighter by the day, yet waves of life can be felt from time to time... I fear something big is about to happen.

t_ch: 果然还是偷偷摸来了啊，万敌。你这副身躯实在显眼，别处也就算了，在云石天宫，不被人注意就怪了。
t_en: So you did come creeping in here after all, Mydei. I don't know about anywhere else, but that conspicuous body of yours is bound to be noticed in Marmoreal Palace.

t_ch: 有何贵干？
t_en: Is there something I can do for you?

t_ch: 没什么，怕你仗着不死之躯不做「入浴」的准备。来了就好。
t_en: Nothing in particular. I feared you wouldn't be preparing to "bathe" with that immortal body of yours. You're here, and that's all that matters.

t_ch: 说来也是怪事——我也以为黑潮根本奈何不了你。
t_en: It is strange, though, now that I say it... I thought that the black tide would have no effect on you.

t_ch: 哦。
t_en: Oh.

t_ch: 看来那力量终归出于「神」授——连它们自身都无法幸免，自然也无法再庇护你。
t_en: It seems that power comes from the "gods" after all... If even they themselves are at its mercy, it is only natural that they are unable to protect you.

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 那么…黑潮是你唯一的弱点？
t_en: So... is the black tide your only weakness?

t_ch: 不。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 看样子你这是在赶我走啊，那不聊这些远的，我还真有些事想请教——
t_en: I see you're trying to get rid of me. I'll change the topic. There is something that I actually wish to ask you...

t_ch: 城中能与我酣战的人没有几位，旅途也并没有那么多挑战非人之物的机会。只因如此，我难以察觉自己剑技的薄弱之处——直到遇见你。
t_en: There are few in this city that can match me in battle, and chances to battle non-humans are few and far between in my travels. Because of this, it has been hard for me to observe the weaknesses in my swordsmanship... Until I met you.

t_ch: 之前与你交手的时候，我的耐力不足，险些因你的痛击弃了剑柄。若非技巧略胜一筹，恐怕真要败给你了。
t_en: When I crossed swords with you before, I lacked endurance and your powerful blows nearly knocked my blade from my hands. If it weren't for my slightly superior technique, I fear you would have defeated me.

t_ch: 技巧？呵，不如说是些可笑的小手段。
t_en: Technique? Hah, you mean your laughable little tricks?

t_ch: 哈——我知道该怎么和你搭上话了。咱们都打过架了，就互换些心得如何？
t_en: Hah. I know how to relate to you now. As we have fought before, how about we share some of our insights?
...Huh?

t_ch: 战斗了那么久，我从未见到你有疲惫之色…你是如何锻炼的，教教我如何？
t_en: We have battled for all this time, yet I have never seen you fatigued... How do you train? Would you consider teaching me?

t_ch: 那你恐怕要失望了，英雄。这不是靠锻炼——
t_en: I fear you will be disappointed, hero. This is not due to my training...

t_ch: 我比你更能忍耐苦楚，仅此而已。
t_en: I am simply more capable of enduring pain and suffering than you are.

t_ch: …没错，尊贵的黄金裔，我恳求您予这可怜的生物一死。
t_en: ...That's correct, honorable Chrysos Heir. I implore you to grant this pitiful creature its death.

t_ch: 因为我们的培育失误，它有着无从修补的缺陷。目前看来，它至多拥有十五日的生命。而这短暂的生命，也要一刻不停地被笼罩在痛苦当中……
t_en: Due to an error in the way that we have nurtured it, it has a defect that cannot be repaired. As things stand, it has at most fifteen days left to live. What's more, it will be in endless agony for the entirety of its short existence.

t_ch: 您也听到了，它那可怜的哀嚎！
t_en: You hear them too, right? Those pitiful screams!

t_ch: 嗯，一切会如你所愿。只是…为什么要让它出生呢？
t_en: Alright, as you wish. I just want to know... Why did you allow it to be born?

t_ch: 万事万物灭绝有其原因，难以扭转。依靠着这些「种子」，你们只是在「创造」，而不是让已经逝去的「重现」。
t_en: Every being that goes extinct does so for a reason, and it is difficult to undo. By relying on these "seeds," you are only "creating," and you are not allowing the dead to "return."

t_ch: 因为…它或许不必面对自然。
t_en: Because... perhaps this way it does not have to face nature.

t_ch: 在尚未灭绝前，这些生灵在漫长时间里都被圈养着，是这片大地上最常见的肉食。
t_en: Before it went extinct, these creatures spent a long time in captivity. They were the most common source of meat in this land.

t_ch: 肉食…它们重返尘世，只为了再一次死去？
t_en: A source of meat... They are returning to the world just to die once more?

t_ch: 黄金裔大人，您在为此责怪我吗？
t_en: Lady Chrysos Heir, are you blaming me for this?

t_ch: 不，或许我和它也没什么不同。
t_en: No. Perhaps this creature and I are one and the same.

t_ch: 请暂且避让，我来结束它的痛苦吧。
t_en: Please move aside. I will bring an end to its suffering.

t_ch: …这当然不难，阿格莱雅。
t_en: ...This is simple, Aglaea.

t_ch: 这种闹市，就算您特意乔装，也太容易被认出来——何况您完全没这样的打算。
t_en: Even if you had disguised yourself, you would be far too easily recognized in a market as busy as this... Not to mention you have no plans to disguise yourself.

t_ch: 人人都知道，收获月里，您总有一天会来到这里，似是与人有约，却又谁也不见。
t_en: Everyone knows that you would come to this place one day during the Month of Reaping. It is as if you had arranged to meet someone, yet you are alone.

t_ch: 你是为此而来？
t_en: Is that why you are here?

t_ch: 只是恰好贪嘴——据说有几位刚到圣城的难民重拾旧业，他们的手艺相当不错，还非常少见。
t_en: I am simply here to satisfy my appetite... I hear that there are recently arrived refugees in the holy city that have resumed their old trades. Their talents are outstanding and not commonly seen in this area.

t_ch: 那你和我…倒算是殊途同归。
t_en: Then you and I... Have taken different routes to reach the same place.

t_ch: 哦？您莫非也……
t_en: Oh? Are you also...

t_ch: 我来观晓命运如何黯淡。
t_en: I am here to see just how bleak fate is.

t_ch: 又或…观晓将会经我之手的纺线，是如何在此处「回响」。
t_en: Or, should I say... Here to see how the threads that will pass through my hands "resonate" here.

t_ch: 您这…哈…我一下子都不知道该如何追问。
t_en: So you... Uhh... I'm not really sure what to say to that.

t_ch: 白厄阁下，那引你而来的手艺，是从何时起，变得如此珍稀呢？
t_en: Lord Phainon, these talents that have brought you here... when did they become so rare?

t_ch: 倒也没有多久，只是……
t_en: Not that long ago, it's just...
Oh...

t_ch: 没错，白厄阁下，一切都在消逝。我之所以来到此处，正是为了「相较」。
t_en: That's right, Lord Phainon. Everything is beginning to fade away. I am here precisely because I wish to "compare."

t_ch: 随着时日推移，又有什么成为了往日的一部分，不复存在。
t_en: With every passing day, something else becomes a part of the past, never to exist again.

t_ch: 我又该如何将它们化作金线，纺织明日。
t_en: How can I turn them into golden threads with which to weave tomorrow?

t_ch: 毕竟，圣城已是人们唯一的庇护之所，在此处你无法寻得的，恐怕已在这世上绝迹。
t_en: The holy city is the only solace for mankind now, after all. I fear that what you cannot find here is already lost to this world.

t_ch: 那么您所纺织的明日，这些能拥有一席之地吗？
t_en: So, will these things have a place in the tomorrow that you weave?

t_ch: …事无巨细。
t_en: ...No matter how big or small?

t_ch: 那便太过贪心。白厄阁下……
t_en: Now, that would be too greedy, Lord Phainon...

t_ch: 当我们走出这集市，请你即刻忘却此事——
t_en: The moment we leave this market, please forget about what I have said...

t_ch: 眼下只是一瞬的「悲观」，又或一瞬的「残忍」。
t_en: What you are seeing is just a moment of "pessimism," or perhaps a moment of "ruthlessness."

t_ch: 命运是如此沉重，或许，我只足以纺织一缕游丝。
t_en: This is the great weight of fate. Perhaps, all I am fit to weave is a sheet of gossamer.

t_ch: 但倘若手上的纺线唯余其一，无论多么出类拔萃的衣匠，即便是我……
t_en: However, if there is only one weaving thread remaining in my hands, it does not matter how talented a garmentmaker I am...

t_ch: …也只能将它制成「提线」。
t_en: ...I can't hope to make it into any more than a "puppet string."

t_ch: 即使已经陨落，塔兰顿的审判，还是如此繁杂。
t_en: Though they have fallen already, the remaining judgments of Talanton are many and varied.

t_ch: 事到如今，想要通过它的试炼竟还要借助<color=#dbc291ff>扎格列斯之手</color>，实在是讽刺。
t_en: It's quite ironic to think that even now, to overcome its challenges, you need the <color=#dbc291ff>Hand of Zagreus</color>.

t_ch: 大人，这种力气活还是我来吧，您对自己的身体应该多点关心的。
t_en: My lord, let me do the heavy work. You must take care of your health.

t_ch: 也罢，还记得该怎么做吗？
t_en: Very well. Do you remember how to do it?

t_ch: 侍奉您这么多年了，这点儿事还是清楚的。把象征世界的<color=#dbc291ff>天球</color>放在<color=#dbc291ff>右侧</color>，对吧？
t_en: After all these years serving you, one thing I'm sure of. The <color=#dbc291ff>celestial sphere</color> that symbolizes the world should be on the <color=#dbc291ff>right</color>, isn't it?

t_ch: 没错，天球砝码在右侧，你我<color=#dbc291ff>站上左侧的托盘</color>，天平便会将我们载上终点。
t_en: That's right, the celestial sphere's weight goes on the right side, and we <color=#dbc291ff>stand on the left tray</color> — the scale will lift us right to the destination.

t_ch: 不过，另一侧的，我倒是从来都没有用过。
t_en: I have never used the other side before, though.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>三角</color>的砝码象征着命运，把它放在<color=#dbc291ff>左侧</color>，<color=#dbc291ff>站在右侧托盘</color>的朝圣者们便能如愿进入神殿深处。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The triangle</color> weights symbolize destiny. Place them on the <color=#dbc291ff>left</color>, <color=#dbc291ff>and those pilgrims standing on the right tray</color> can venture deep into the temple.

t_ch: 切记，天球在右，三角在左，受审判者则站在相反的位置。错误的砝码，只会让天平保持沉默。
t_en: Remember: The celestial sphere goes on the right and the triangle goes on the left. The person undergoing judgment stands on the opposite side. If you place the wrong weight in the wrong location, the scales will remain silent.

t_ch: 那如果我在两侧都放上砝码呢？
t_en: What if I place weights on both sides of the scales?

t_ch: 天平将回归平衡，就像两侧都未盛放砝码那样，过度的贪婪与空无一物，二者并无差别。
t_en: The scales will return to perfect balance, as if there is no weight on them at all. Greed and nothingness are inseparable in nature.

t_ch: 真严苛，就像公正之秤仍在看着我们一样。
t_en: The rules are so harsh. It's as if the Scale of Justice is still watching over us.

t_ch: 走吧，但愿帕西提亚大人能挺过来。
t_en: Let's go. Hope Lady Pasithea can make it.

t_ch: 进入神殿者，当受天平审判。
t_en: Enter the temple and be judged by the divine scale.

t_ch: 借助<color=#dbc291ff>扎格列斯之手</color>，把象征世界的<color=#dbc291ff>天球</color>放在<color=#dbc291ff>右侧</color>。
t_en: With the <color=#dbc291ff>Hand of Zagreus</color>, place the <color=#dbc291ff>celestial sphere</color> that symbolizes the world on the <color=#dbc291ff>right</color>.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>站上左侧的托盘</color>，天平会将无罪者载上终点。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Step onto the left tray</color>, and the scales will lift the innocent all the way to the destination.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>三角</color>的砝码象征命运，把它放在<color=#dbc291ff>左侧</color>，<color=#dbc291ff>站在右侧托盘</color>的朝圣者便能如愿进入神殿深处。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The triangle</color> weight symbolizes destiny. Place it on the <color=#dbc291ff>left</color>, <color=#dbc291ff>and those pilgrims standing on the right tray</color> can venture deep into the temple.

t_ch: 圣女大人，您又来采火山玻璃了么？
t_en: Great Holy Maiden, you are here to collect volcanic glass again?

t_ch: 是的，最近城里总是需要这东西…实在不是好事。
t_en: Indeed, there is constant need for it in the city at the moment... Things are truly dire.

t_ch: 现在情况如何？都说政局挺动荡的，不会要打仗吧？
t_en: How is the city faring at the moment? People are saying there is unrest in politics. You don't think it will lead to war, do you?

t_ch: 圣女大人，您偷摸给我点口信，要真不对劲，我还想着带我家老小逃奔个安宁地方过日子呢。
t_en: Great Holy Maiden, you must tell me. If there really is unrest, I would like to take my family somewhere safe to live out their days.

t_ch: 哈哈…朋友，要是真的打仗了，恐怕跑到哪里都安全不了吧。
t_en: Haha... My friend. If it really came to war, I fear there would be no safe place to run to.

t_ch: 也是。喏，圣女大人，这枚火山玻璃拿去吧，别再自己下矿区亲手去采了。
t_en: This is true. Ah, great Holy Maiden, take this volcanic glass with you. Please don't go down to the mines to source it yourself anymore.

t_ch: 那些红水晶原矿的断裂面太锋利了，每次见您双手鲜血淋漓地离开，我心里总挺难受。
t_en: The fractured surfaces of those red crystals are far too sharp. My heart hurts every time I see your hands dripping with blood as you leave.

t_ch: …谢谢你。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 对了，下回您再需要这些石头，直接和我说声就行！别看我一副大忙人的样子，偶尔开小差凿几块火山玻璃还是不在话下的。
t_en: Come to think of it, just let me know the next time you need some ore! I may seem busy, but I can always make time to carve out some volcanic glass for you.

t_ch: 不必麻烦了…这可能是我最后一次来取火山玻璃了。
t_en: There is no need for you to go to such trouble... This is likely the last time I shall come to collect volcanic glass.

t_ch: 明白了，圣女大人，您多保重。我听人说，那群达官贵人待你态度总不太好……
t_en: I understand, great Holy Maiden. Please take care of yourself. I have heard word that those officials have been treating you poorly...

t_ch: 实在熬不住，您就开溜！翁法罗斯这么大…唉，您瞧我，又开始说这回事儿了。
t_en: If it becomes too much for you to bear, you must escape! Amphoreus is so vast... Ah, there I go talking about these things again.

t_ch: 谢谢你的好意。或许我本可以逃开，但…有些时候，命运看似给你开启了好几扇门扉，其实能走的，只有一条路径吧。
t_en: I appreciate your intentions. Perhaps I could run away, but... Sometimes it seems as if although fate has opened many doors to you, there is still only one path that you can actually walk.

t_ch: 呵，今年的悬锋祭典还真不一般——唯一突破重围杀到朕面前的，竟是一名如此年轻、甚至有些纤弱的斗士？
t_en: Hah, the Kremnos Festival is really something this year... To think that the only person to break through the siege and fight their way to me was such a young and rather frail-looking warrior?

t_ch: 王欧利庞，切莫小看了你眼前这名纤弱的斗士。我名为歌耳戈，此行前来只为摘取那唯一的桂冠。哪怕对手是您，我的长矛也绝不会有半分踌躇！
t_en: King Eurypon, do not underestimate this frail-looking warrior who stands before you. My name is Gorgo. I have come to claim the one wreath. Even if it is you who stands against me, my spear will not hesitate.

t_ch: 名为歌耳戈？哈哈！你的父母也着实足够狂妄，岂不知此乃悬锋建城者的名讳？
t_en: Your name is Gorgo? Haha! Your parents have some arrogance! Do they not know that was the name of the one that built Kremnos?

t_ch: 这名字并非源于父母。我曾徒手搏杀过一头雄狮，与「搦狮之王」歌耳戈立下了相似的伟业。
t_en: It was not my parents who named me this. I once killed a lion with my bare hands, achieving a feat similar to that of Gorgo: "Conqueror of Lions."

t_ch: 那以后我便将名字改成了歌耳戈。我将效仿先王，以我的鲜血开创悬锋城崭新的历史辉光！
t_en: From that moment on, I changed my name to Gorgo. I will follow the example of that previous ruler and carve out a glorious new future for Castrum Kremnos with my own blood!

t_ch: 很好…那么，歌耳戈，举矛吧！
t_en: Very well... Then raise your spear, Gorgo!

t_ch: 歌耳戈的长矛与欧利庞的长剑锋刃相接，如同两尊尼卡多利的分身沙场角力，血尘四散。
t_en: The spear of Gorgo and the blade of Eurypon clashed, and they struggled against each other on the battlefield like two clones of Nikador, as blood and dust streaked through the air.

t_ch: 数回合后，二人各自停手，欧利庞大腿遭长矛洞穿，而歌耳戈脖颈上的伤口再深一毫就能要了她的命。
t_en: After a few rounds of battle, both warriors came to a halt. Eurypon's thigh had been pierced by Gorgo's spear, and the wound on Gorgo's throat threatened to take her life if it grew an inch deeper.

t_ch: 歌耳戈，真是出乎朕的意料。
t_en: Gorgo, you have truly exceeded my expectations.

t_ch: 不愧是王…如今您难以动弹，而我武器脱手，恐怕只能算作平局。但想必，那顶桂冠，我应有资格摘得了吧？
t_en: Just what I would expect of a king... Now that you cannot move, and my weapon is out of my hands, I fear that we must call this a draw. Though I'm certain that I have earned myself that wreath, have I not?

t_ch: 王，若您意犹未尽…便以王之名，取我头颅吧——但这与此次胜负无关。战斗已经结束，我不愿徒增无谓的鲜血。
t_en: My king, if you are not yet satisfied... Then take my head as the king... However, this has nothing to do with victory or defeat. The battle is over, and I just simply do not wish to shed meaningless blood.

t_ch: …朕并非此意。刚才，若你在我手下坚持十合未倒，那么这顶桂冠与你正登对。
t_en: ...You misunderstand me. Just now, if you had managed to last ten rounds against me, the crown would be rightfully yours.

t_ch: 若你的长矛洞穿了我的咽喉或者心脏，那么便将王冠拿去，让悬锋新王浴血代冠。
t_en: If your spear had pierced my throat or my heart, you could have taken the crown and been bathed in the blood of Kremnos as the new ruler.

t_ch: 可如今，我俩身手相仿、意志相匹，同样沐浴在尼卡多利的辉光之下，将那五大美德煊赫播扬……
t_en: However, we are matched in both skill and will. Bathed in the light of Nikador, we both sow the seeds of the five great virtues.

t_ch: ——歌耳戈，你可愿戴上悬锋王妃的金冠，持矛举盾相伴身侧，与朕一同拱卫悬锋城的荣耀？
t_en: ...Gorgo, would you wear the crown as Queen of Kremnos, wield your spear and shield by my side, and defend the glory of Castrum Kremnos with me?

t_ch: 于此「天谴之矛」神殿、尼卡多利淬炼神锋之处，我等将举行加冕仪式。
t_en: We will hold a coronation ceremony here, in the Lance of Fury's temple where Nikador tempered the divine blade.

t_ch: 悬锋勇士克拉特鲁斯，于祭典中昂扬战意、蹀血搏杀，彰显悬锋之五大美德——
t_en: Krateros, warrior of Kremnos, has demonstrated the five great virtues of Kremnos during this festival, fighting with great spirit and spreading blood and death.

t_ch: 不惧锋刃悬喉，以彰勇气之形貌；
t_en: Unfearing of blade at the throat, manifesting the visage of courage.

t_ch: 不屑诡计翻覆，以卫荣耀之冠冕；
t_en: Unyielding to conniving treachery, protecting the crown of honor.

t_ch: 不闭炯炯双眸，以守理智之本性；
t_en: Unblinking of eyes burning bright, upholding the cornerstone of reason.

t_ch: 不伏鳞伤之躯，以铸坚韧之品格；
t_en: Unbending from wounds to flesh, forging characters of tenacity.

t_ch: 不惮搏命拱卫，以显牺牲之傲骨。
t_en: Undaunted of risking life to protect, embodying corpus of sacrifice.

t_ch: ——特赐以督战勇士之荣誉。克拉特鲁斯，你可愿担下此名？
t_en: ...I bestow unto you the title of Watcher of Warriors. Krateros, will you accept this name?

t_ch: 荣幸之至。克拉特鲁斯必不惜此身，如铸铁羽翼卫于王侧，坚守悬锋荣耀。
t_en: It would be my greatest honor. I, Krateros, would give my life and defend your side, like iron wings that hold tight the honor of Kremnos.

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯，你在欧利庞手下坚持了三十余合，于众督战勇士中亦最为骁勇，我以悬锋王后之名，将一项特殊的职责托付予你……
t_en: Krateros. You stood tall over thirty rounds against the hand of Eurypon. You stand as the most courageous of all warriors. As Queen of Kremnos, I bestow unto you a special duty...

t_ch: 我腹中的王子，迈德漠斯，数月后将来到这个世界。他继承了王欧利庞与我的铮铮铁骨，又日夜沐浴在尼卡多利的神性照耀之下，必将成为新的悬锋之王。
t_en: Mydeimos, the prince that I carry in my body, will be born to this world in a number of months. He has inherited the iron bones of myself and King Eurypon and been bathed in the divine light of Nikador day and night. He must become the new King of Kremnos.

t_ch: 日后，你可愿做王师，授其武艺、护其不受毒计迫害，待其成王，便如拱卫王欧利庞一般，用生命捍卫迈德漠斯的王冠？
t_en: When that time comes, would you become his mentor? Teach him martial arts, protect him from malicious plots, and give your life to protect his throne when he becomes king, as you have with King Eurypon?

t_ch: 这…王后如此屈尊恳切相托，我又怎能令您失望？
t_en: This... My Queen, I would not dream of disappointing you with such an earnest request.

t_ch: 呵，歌耳戈，你就如此相信迈德漠斯能从朕手里夺走王冠么？
t_en: Hah. Gorgo, are you so certain that Mydeimos will be able to take the crown from my hands?

t_ch: 欧利庞，不要说笑了，你比谁都相信迈德漠斯。
t_en: Do not be ridiculous, Eurypon. You have more faith in Mydeimos than anyone.

t_ch: 哈哈哈！什么都瞒不过你。如果说世界上有什么人能将我杀死，那只有我与你的孩子了。
t_en: Hahaha! I cannot hide anything from you. If there were anyone in this world that could kill me, it could only ever be our child.

t_ch: 若他果真身负悬锋城的未来，朕这颗头颅就是让他拿去又如何？
t_en: If he will truly bear the future of Castrum Kremnos, what does it matter if he takes my head?

t_ch: 闭嘴，欧利庞，你想让他像你一样，也犯下弑父之罪么？我与你做的约定，你可不要忘了。
t_en: Silence, Eurypon. You wish for him to commit patricide like yourself? You are not to forget the promise you made me.

t_ch: 咳，说笑，说笑。克拉特鲁斯，督战勇士中的魁首，今后你就是迈德漠斯的师傅了。必要时候，你可忠于他胜过忠于我。
t_en: Ahem, I was kidding, just kidding! Krateros, master primus of the Watchers. From this day forward, you shall be Mydeimos' mentor. When the time calls, your loyalty to him shall supersede your loyalty to even me.

t_ch: 是，欧利庞王、歌耳戈王后。
t_en: Very well, King Eurypon, Queen Gorgo.

t_ch: 尊贵的王，无论如何重复预言的步骤，都会是一样的结局……
t_en: Honorable king, it does not matter how you repeat the steps of the prophecy, the outcome will always be the same...

t_ch: 预言已经不容置喙地指明，您的孩子迈德漠斯，将夺走您的性命、断绝尼卡多利的永世长存，甚至悬锋王朝，都将在他手中终结……
t_en: The prophecy has already stated without question that your child, Mydeimos, will take your life, bring an end to Nikador's eternal existence, and even the dynasty of Kremnos will fall at his hands...

t_ch: 他将成为亡国的王储。
t_en: He will become the crown prince of a vanquished nation.

t_ch: 王欧利庞，您还在犹豫什么！想象我们先前构想的伟业，您是永远的王，悬锋城将万载延续…与这相比，一名婴孩的性命又算得了什么？
t_en: King Eurypon, what are you waiting for!? Imagine the great undertaking that we conceived. You will be the eternal King and Castrum Kremnos will continue on for millennia... What is the life of a single baby compared to all of this?

t_ch: 放肆。那是我与歌耳戈的骨肉，几时轮得到你非议？
t_en: Insolence! That child is mine and Gorgo's flesh and blood. When did you gain the right to criticize my actions?

t_ch: 王欧利庞，请息怒。
t_en: King Eurypon, please quell your rage.

t_ch: …不过，道理朕是明白的。如今悬锋看似鼎盛，背后却危机四伏。在未名之敌黑潮的侵袭之下，就连尼卡多利也逐渐失神。
t_en: ...I understand your reasoning. Though Castrum Kremnos seems to be prospering on the surface, behind the scenes there is danger all around. Even Nikador is gradually losing their mind under the attacks of our faceless enemy, the black tide.

t_ch: 为了悬锋城的永续…已无回头路可走了。
t_en: For the eternal future of Castrum Kremnos... I have no other choice.

t_ch: 王，您的决断将功惠万世。只是，我还有一事忧虑……
t_en: My king, the generations shall prosper from your verdict. However, there is one concern that remains...

t_ch: 朕知道你在想什么——歌耳戈，像她这般坚定善战的斗士，绝不会坐视我们将迈德漠斯杀死。
t_en: I know what you are thinking... A determined and talented warrior like Gorgo will not simply sit back and watch as we take Mydeimos' life.

t_ch: 想必她会提出挑战王权的动议，以她最擅长的方式——角斗——保护她的孩子。
t_en: I am certain that she will challenge me for the throne and try to protect her child using what she is best at... gladiatorial combat.

t_ch: 我并非不相信您的武力，只是，歌耳戈王后的身手向来不在您之下，万一发生意外，我们所有殚精竭虑的筹划都将化为泡影。
t_en: It is not that I do not have faith in your martial prowess. It is just that Queen Gorgo has always been a match for you. If the unexpected were to happen, all of our well-laid plans would vanish into thin air.

t_ch: 我等愿不惜性命，想办法刺杀歌耳戈王后……
t_en: We are willing to sacrifice our lives and find a way to assassinate Queen Gorgo...

t_ch: 呵…歌耳戈，再怎么说也是悬锋的王后，我的妻子。下作的手段，便由我来做吧。
t_en: Hah... Say what you will, but Gorgo is the Queen of Kremnos and my wife. I shall be the one to execute this foul deed.

t_ch: 替朕备好毒药，不要太烈，能稍微麻痹筋骨足矣。临行那日，我会设法让她饮下。
t_en: Prepare a poison. Do not make it too strong, just enough to numb her body. I shall find a way to have her drink it the day that I leave.

t_ch: 我与歌耳戈是在血尘弥漫的角斗中相识相爱…那么，便也让我在角斗中，亲手与她作别吧。
t_en: Gorgo and I met and fell in love in the blood and dust of battle... So, allow me to bid her farewell personally in battle once more.

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯，您当真要离开悬锋城？如今悬锋正值鼎盛，王欧利庞对你又颇为重用，光是王翼冠军的名号就足使万人景仰了。
t_en: Krateros, are you really going to leave Castrum Kremnos? Kremnos is prospering and King Eurypon places great importance on you. Just the title of King's Aegis is enough to win the admiration of thousands.

t_ch: 伊格尼斯，你只知督战勇士魁首、王翼冠军之名，却不知我还有另一重同样光荣的名号——
t_en: Ignis, you only know me by my titles of champion warrior and King's Aegis. What you do not know is that I bear another, equally glorious title...

t_ch: 那便是歌耳戈王后亲允的王师之名。
t_en: That is the title of King's mentor, granted to me by Queen Gorgo.

t_ch: …我不懂您的意思。
t_en: ...I don't understand what you are saying.

t_ch: 在我像你一样，还是个毛头小子的时候，歌耳戈曾与我共事狩猎，我亲眼目睹了她搏杀雄狮的壮景。
t_en: When I was a sprout like you, I hunted alongside Gorgo. With my own eyes, I saw her kill a lion with her bare hands.

t_ch: 那时歌耳戈还不是王后，只是一名威严却不失亲切的斗士。她待部族内所有的孩童都如己出，我也有幸蒙她教导，学了一招半式，这才能在悬锋祭典中胜出。
t_en: Back then, Gorgo was not yet queen. She was merely a ferocious but kind warrior. She treated every child in the tribe as her own. I was also fortunate enough to receive her tutelage and learned skills that I would not have claimed victory in the Kremnos Festival without.

t_ch: 在我成为督战勇士的那天，歌耳戈看我的眼神，竟与以往并无二致…在她眼里，我还是一名亲切的旧友。
t_en: The day I became a Watcher, Gorgo looked me in the eyes exactly as she did back then... In her eyes, I was still her dear old friend.

t_ch: 歌耳戈王后将她的孩子，迈德漠斯，托付给了我，我曾在尼卡多利的目光下发誓要用性命守护悬锋的王子……
t_en: Queen Gorgo entrusted her child, Mydeimos, to me. I vowed under the light of Nikador that I would protect the Prince of Kremnos with my life...

t_ch: 然而，王欧利庞受奸人蛊惑，意欲杀死亲生骨肉。歌耳戈王后俯身悲呼之时，我却不敢出手，垂着头颅、一言不发。
t_en: However, King Eurypon has been bewitched by traitors and wishes his own flesh and blood dead. When Queen Gorgo doubled over and cried out in agony, I did not dare make a move. I hung my head and stilled my tongue.

t_ch: 为了保护迈德漠斯，歌耳戈王后向王欧利庞发起了角斗，不幸落败，被一剑割开了咽喉。
t_en: Queen Gorgo challenged King Eurypon to gladiator combat to protect Mydeimos. Unfortunately, she was defeated and had her throat slit by his blade.

t_ch: 收殓她尸身之时，我却在她身上发现了中毒的症状……
t_en: But when I examined her body, I found symptoms of poisoning...

t_ch: 她是死于奸计！
t_en: She fell to an evil plot!

t_ch: 我愧对歌耳戈王后。如今，悬锋城外有一股孤军流窜，据说首领是拒绝死亡之人。我要去寻那孤军，我相信…那是迈德漠斯回来了。
t_en: I failed Queen Gorgo. Now, I hear that Castrum Kremnos has a detachment army roaming outside the city. Rumor has it that their leader is one that defied death. Krateros. I am going to find that lone army. I believe... Mydeimos has returned.

t_ch: 迈德漠斯，你做到了，我们做到了！我们成功地替你的母亲，替歌耳戈王后复仇了。
t_en: Mydeimos, you did it! We did it! We have taken revenge for your mother! We have avenged Queen Gorgo!

t_ch: 现在，只差最后一步——戴上王冠，完成你母亲的遗愿，成为新的悬锋之王。
t_en: Now, there is but one thing remaining... Put on the crown, fulfill your mother's wishes, and become the new King of Kremnos!

t_ch: 我曾向你的母亲发誓，用生命捍卫你的王冠。在你成王之后，我将做你的铸铁双翼，你若想统一翁法罗斯，我永远会是冲在最前面的那个！
t_en: I once vowed to your mother that I would give my life to protect your reign. Once you become king, I will be your iron wings. If you wish to unite Amphoreus, I will always be the one who charges ahead of you into battle!

t_ch: 吾师，莫要忘了我们如今的位置。
t_en: Master, do not forget the position that we are currently in.

t_ch: 我没有忘。寄人篱下，做那金发僭主挥之即来的剑锋，这日子总会有个头的。
t_en: I have not forgotten. We serve as the mercenary blade of the blonde tyrant, dependent on her for our existence. However, these days shall come to an end.

t_ch: 但绝不是现在。
t_en: But that moment is not now.

t_ch: …我理解你的心情，少主。你刚刚才亲手杀死了你父亲，心情复杂沉重，这无可厚非。
t_en: ...I understand how you feel, young master. You just now killed your own father. It is to be expected that your emotions are complicated and heavy.

t_ch: 但是，踏着父辈的尸身沐血代冠，这正是悬锋千年未变的传统……
t_en: However... Standing on the body of your fathers, bathing in their blood, and then putting on the crown is a Kremnoan tradition that has continued for millennia...

t_ch: 吾师，我且问你：我的母亲，果真是这般期许我的么？弑父夺位，继续那无休无止的征伐之路？
t_en: Master, let me ask you: Did my mother really have such expectations of me? Did she wish for me to commit patricide, to steal the throne, and continue this endless conquest?

t_ch: 此事休要再提，我心中有数。现在，班师回城吧，我们有太多阵亡的战士需要祭奠了。
t_en: Do not mention this matter again. I understand the situation. Now, let us return to the city. We have far too many fallen soldiers to honor.

t_ch: 明白了，少主。只是，容我再多嘴一句：悬锋孤军中不乏像我这样已经将你视作新王的人，你只需一声令下，悬锋孤军便会举矛为你掷命。
t_en: I understand, young master. Please just allow me one final word: There are many like myself in the Kremnoan Detachment who already see you as the new King. You need only give the order, and the Kremnoan Detachment would raise their spears and give their lives for you.

t_ch: 莫要让你的母亲、你的族人们失望啊，迈德漠斯。
t_en: Do not let your mother or your people down, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 你记住了「浪漫、纷争与一捆丝线的故事」
t_en: You remembered the "tale of Romance, Strife, and a Bundle of Threads."

t_ch: 你记住了「律法、权衡与一场辩驳的故事」
t_en: You remembered the "tale of Law, Balancing, and a Debate."

t_ch: 你记住了「预言、谎言与一对母女的故事」
t_en: You remembered the "tale of Prophecy, Lies, and a Mother-Daughter Story."

t_ch: 你记住了「反抗传统的情侣与注定流离的命运」
t_en: You remembered "The Lovers in Defiance of Tradition and Their Doomed Fate to Wander"

t_ch: 你记住了「神秘的召唤仪式与始终沉默的巨树」
t_en: You remembered "The Secret Summoning Ceremony and the Ever-Silent Giant Tree"

t_ch: 你记住了「集市商贩的吆喝声与矛盾的最强对决」
t_en: You remembered "The City Merchants' Clamoring and a Duel Between the Strongest Spear and Shield"

t_ch: 你记住了「失去故乡的女孩与她重新踏上的道路」
t_en: You remembered "The Girl Who Lost Her Home and The New Path She Took"

t_ch: 你记住了「消失的探险家与藏在言语背后的诡计」
t_en: You remembered "The Missing Explorer and the Ruse Behind the Words"

t_ch: 你记住了「激烈的修辞学论辩与那座看不见的城邦」
t_en: You remembered "The Intense Rhetorical Debate and that Unseeable City"

t_ch: 你记住了「曾点燃过圣烛」
t_en: You remembered "Lighting the Holy Candle."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在明亮时得到过火柴」
t_en: You remembered "Receiving Matches When It Was Bright."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾回答过真言狮口的提问」
t_en: You remembered "Answering Verax Leo's Questions."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在福坛获得过赐福」
t_en: You remembered "Receiving a Blessing at the Altar."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾使用过欧洛尼斯的祷言」
t_en: You remembered "Using the Prayers of Oronyx."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾乘坐过大地兽」
t_en: You remembered "Riding Dromas."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾打碎过雕塑以及引发的某起事件」
t_en: You remembered "Breaking a Sculpture and the Incident it Caused."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在餐厅品尝过美食」
t_en: You remembered "Tasting Delicacies in the Restaurant."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在果蔬摊品尝过水果」
t_en: You remembered "Tasting Fruits at the Fruit Stall."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在浴场接待员处购买过商品」
t_en: You remembered "Purchasing Items at the Baths Reception."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾与阿格莱雅交谈过」
t_en: You remembered "Talking with Aglaea."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾与缇宝交谈过」
t_en: You remembered "Talking with Tribbie."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾与万敌交谈过」
t_en: You remembered "Talking with Mydei."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾与遐蝶交谈过」
t_en: You remembered "Talking with Castorice."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾在浴场畅饮过秘酿」
t_en: You remembered "Drinking Ambrosia in the Baths."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾驻足聆听过水漫琴的乐声」
t_en: You remembered "Listening Intently to the Music of the Water Lyre."

t_ch: 你记住了「曾倾听过利波吟唱的诗篇」
t_en: You remembered "Listening to Lipos Singing Poems."

t_ch: 残像们所处的位置有些古怪…也许过去此处的环境与现在有所不同。
t_en: The placement of the afterimages seems a little off... Perhaps the environment here is different now to how it once was.

t_ch: 你记起了「浴宫生活的点滴与过往之河的碎片」
t_en: You remember the "fragments of the life in the bath chamber and pieces at the river of the past."

t_ch: 沉重的门扉无法推动，门上涟漪般的纹路中心镶嵌着一个神秘符号，或许这和什么预言有关。真麻烦，早知道在贝洛伯格的时候，就该找老桑博学个开锁速成术。
t_en: A heavy door resists your push. At its center, rippling patterns surround a mysterious symbol, possibly tied to some prophecy. What a hassle — if only you'd learned lock-picking from old Sampo back in Belobog.

t_ch: 交错的枝叶图案中藏着一个预言装置的凹槽，周围的金色纹路仿佛在等待某种启示。 预言家们总是这样——连放个宝贝都要先征得命运的同意。
t_en: Intricate leaf patterns conceal a recess for a prophetic device. Golden engravings around it seem to await some revelation — typical of prophets, seeking destiny's approval even to store treasures.

t_ch: 破碎的茧壳中依然流淌着微弱的光芒，就像墨涅塔留下的叹息，夜晚的花园似乎还记得那个追逐理性之光的蝴蝶，甘愿放弃永恒，只为换取片刻的相拥。
t_en: From the broken cocoon, faint light still emanates, like a sigh left behind by Mnestia. The garden at night seems to remember the butterfly that once chased the light of reason, willingly forsaking eternity for a fleeting embrace.

t_ch: 金色碎片在黑夜中依然明亮，照亮了周围盛开的蓝花。据说当年墨涅塔的蝶群就是从这样的茧中破茧而出，为世间带来了回忆与预言的馈赠。
t_en: Golden fragments remain radiant in the darkness, illuminating the blooming blue flowers around them. It is said that Mnestia's swarm of butterflies once emerged from such cocoons, bestowing the gifts of memory and prophecy upon the world.

t_ch: 向金丝与根系交汇间，泰坦的蝶褪悬置于此。金蝶如潮，鳞粉似流光于血液中播撒「亲族之爱」。
t_en: Where golden threads intertwine with roots, the Titan's exuviate rests here. Golden butterflies flow like tides, their shimmering scale dust spreading "a love between kin" through the bloodstreams they touch.

t_ch: 此乃进步之阶梯、思想漫游之庭园；求道者应于此敬拜，忆其亲族之爱，则求索真理之路畅行无碍。
t_en: This is the stairway of progress, the garden for intellectual wanderers. Seekers of truth should worship here, reflecting on the love for kin, for only through this remembrance may the path to wisdom remain unobstructed.

t_ch: 人当思辨——其求知如捻线作茧，自我裹缠；唯相互砥砺、反复锤炼，智慧之蝶方得破茧，而真理自现。
t_en: Humankind must ponder: Is the pursuit of knowledge not like spinning a cocoon, wrapping oneself in layers? Only through mutual sharpening and repeated tempering can the butterfly of wisdom break free, and truth reveal itself.

t_ch: 据说，世间所有的知识就像一颗大树——它的每一条枝杈代表每一个具体领域的知识；而它们的基底是完全连通的，那树根的名字便叫做「我」。
t_en: It is said that all the world's knowledge resembles a grand tree — Every branch represents a specific field of knowledge, yet all are connected at the base. And the root is "me."

t_ch: 绳结学派有这样一个比喻——我们的种种感受就像树根，经过意志的树干，生发成知识的树冠…但在此之外，才是漫无边际的无知之海。
t_en: The Nodists have a metaphor: Our varied perceptions are like roots, which, through the trunk of will, grow into the treetop of knowledge... But beyond this lies the boundless sea of ignorance.

t_ch: 象征着秩序与和谐的彩色玻璃…有一瞬间你很想听听把它打碎的声音。这种可怕的想法让你猛摇脑袋——「我这是中了什么邪？又被纳努克瞥视了？」
t_en: The stained glass symbolizes order and harmony... For a fleeting moment, you really want to hear the sound of it breaking. This dreadful thought makes you shake your head violently — "What's gotten into me? Have I been glimpsed by Nanook again?"

t_ch: 很难界定这种类似路灯的物品究竟算是动物还是植物——它甚至还在这里承担了重要的交通功能。有些人称呼它为「狄沃努索」…不知道是否哪位贤者在喝醉时取出来的名字。
t_en: It's hard to define whether this lamp-like entity is animal or plant — Here, it even plays a vital role in traffic management. Some refer to it as "Dionyso"... perhaps a name conjured by a drunken sage.

t_ch: 据说有喝醉的贤者曾经在上面题过一行字——「你们想升高时，就向上仰望。我向下俯视，因为我已经升高。」…可能是在描述这种类似路灯的生物的升降功能。
t_en: It is said that a drunken sage once inscribed a line on it: "When you seek to rise, look up. I look down, for I have already risen." Perhaps it's describing the lamp-like creature's lifting and lowering function.

t_ch: 在某个广为流传的童话中，英勇的冒险者顺着豆茎偷走了天上巨人的种种宝贝，最后还杀死了它。面对着如今的树庭，你忽然意识到这个故事对于天上的巨人实在很不友好。
t_en: In a widely told fairy tale, a brave adventurer climbed a beanstalk to steal treasures from the giant in the sky, ultimately killing it. Standing now in the Grove, you suddenly realize how unkind this story is to the giants above.

t_ch: 据说银河中最大的水力发电机修建在一颗被潮汐锁定的星球上——在晨昏线上，融化的冰川水永无止境地流向它们沸腾的方向。不知道{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}全世之座{RUBY_E#}会不会对这种工程项目有兴趣。
t_en: It is said that the Cosmos' largest hydroelectric station was built on a tidally locked planet — At the terminator line, melting glaciers endlessly flow towards their boiling destinations. One wonders if {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}the Throne of Worlds{RUBY_E#} would find such engineering feats interesting.

t_ch: 「水车无法汲取已经流去的水」——如今空无一人的树庭令人想起这句逝去王者的谚语。你突然产生了灵性的一问：再创世的图景真的符合热力学第二定律吗？
t_en: "The waterwheel cannot draw water that has already flowed away." The now-deserted Grove evokes this adage from a bygone king. You suddenly ponder a profound question: Does the vision of Era Nova truly align with the Second Law of Thermodynamics?

t_ch: 在星际间的不少文明中，「巨大的轮子」都作为「命运」的隐喻而存在着。「每种相似的故事都至少会发生两次——一次作为严肃的喜剧，一次作为荒诞的悲剧。」
t_en: In many interstellar civilizations, "giant wheels" symbolize "fate." "Every similar story will occur at least twice — once as a solemn comedy, and once as an absurd tragedy."

t_ch: 金丝与根系交错间，泰坦的心脏悬置于此。神血交融，纯粹之理性方得知晓何为「友邻之爱」。
t_en: The golden threads and roots intertwine, and the Titan's heart is suspended here. With divine blood mingling, only pure reason can grasp the meaning of "neighborly love."

t_ch: 此乃知识汇流处、真理之海的入口；求道者应于此敬拜，忆其友邻之爱，则求索真理之门訇然中开。
t_en: This is the confluence of knowledge, the gateway to the sea of truth. Seekers should worship here, reflecting on the love for neighbors, for only then may the gates of wisdom swing open.

t_ch: 树庭中每有灵感的火花乍现，巨木便为这思想的新生而悄然欢庆——露水凝聚、滴落池中，如丝雨润海、息吹入风。
t_en: Every spark of inspiration that arises within the Grove brings silent celebration from the giant tree toward this birth of thought — Dew gathers and drips into the pool, like silken rain nourishing the ocean, or breaths of wind infusing the air.

t_ch: 据说学者们设计这些荷叶浮台的初心，是为了让荷叶上的露水能洗涤求知者的困惑。不过现在看来，它们更像是漂浮在废墟上的救生筏。
t_en: It is said that the original intent behind designing these lotus leaf platforms was to let the dew upon them cleanse the doubts of seekers. But now, they seem more like lifeboats drifting amidst ruins.

t_ch: 原本清澈的瀑布飞流此刻泛着淡淡幽蓝，飞溅的水珠像是无声的眼泪坠入水池，恍惚间你听到几声叹息。或许这就是为什么这里叫做「垂泪净身室」。
t_en: The once-clear waterfall now glimmers faintly with a bluish hue, its splashing droplets falling like silent tears into the pool below. In a trance, you think you hear faint sighs. Perhaps this is why the place is called the "Chamber of Purifying Tears."

t_ch: 水池中的圆形台座表面似乎刻着某种符文，据说这是瑟希斯写下的终极问题。不过此刻，只有些许涟漪在诉说它的寂寞，瑟希斯留下的问题终究成了未解之谜。
t_en: The circular pedestal in the pool appears to bear some kind of runic inscriptions. Legend has it that these are the ultimate questions penned by Cerces. Yet now only ripples remain to tell of its solitude — the questions left by Cerces have ultimately become unsolved mysteries.

t_ch: 布满裂纹的书架上，散落着数不清的卷轴，曾经用以精心捆绑的金线已经风化断裂，你深刻地感受到什么叫岁月无情。
t_en: Cracked shelves hold countless scrolls. The gold threads once used to meticulously bind the volumes are all now decayed and broken, making you keenly feel relentless time's cruelty.

t_ch: 被岁月染成暗金色的卷轴，似乎记载着命运织者们留下的重要启示。你取下一卷，发现上面的标题赫然是——《给预言家的<unbreak>101</unbreak>个开场白》。
t_en: The scrolls, aged to a dark golden hue by time, seem to hold vital revelations left behind by the Weavers of Destiny. You pull one out and its title immediately catches your eye: 101 Opening Lines for Prophets.

t_ch: 能放进圣殿书架上的卷轴，想必是什么珍贵的典籍。可惜你找到的这一卷，竟是《五年占卜三天速成教程》。
t_en: The scrolls housed in a temple library must surely be precious tomes, yet the one you find turns out to be A Three-Day Crash Course of Divination.

t_ch: 你不甘心地翻了翻旁边的几卷，分别是《命运织者历年重大预言集》、《圣女不会告诉你的预言秘诀》和《危机破解术：重塑你的占卜价值》，你险些被信徒们积年的焦虑所压垮。
t_en: Unwilling to give up, you sift through a few others nearby. Their titles read: Major Prophecies of the Weavers of Destiny, Secrets the Holy Maiden Won't Share About Prophecy, and Crisis Decoding: Reshaping Your Divination Value. You nearly buckle under the anxiety accumulated over generations by the devout.

t_ch: 静立的石像仿佛披着一层薄纱，周围点缀着发光的昆虫与幽蓝色的花朵。这景象莫名让人想起墨涅塔化蝶的传说。
t_en: The silent statue seems shrouded in a delicate veil, surrounded by glowing insects and bluish flowers. The scene inexplicably brings to mind Mnestia's legend of transforming into butterflies.

t_ch: 原来在化作蝴蝶之前，它也曾是个喜欢独自散步的孤独夜游者。
t_en: Before becoming butterflies, it seems they too were a solitary nocturnal wanderer who enjoyed solitary strolls.

t_ch: 石像低垂的兜帽下空无一物，却让人感觉仿佛被注视着。四周的萤火虫无惧这份庄严，依然在它身边欢快地起舞。
t_en: Beneath the statue's lowered hood is an empty void, yet it gives one the eerie feeling of being watched. The fireflies dancing joyfully around it seem undeterred by its solemnity.

t_ch: 看来就连永夜的信徒，也无法抗拒瑟希斯花园的魔力。
t_en: It seems even followers of the eternal night cannot resist the enchantment of the garden of Cerces.

t_ch: 青草已悄然爬上雕像的衣褶，蓝花在它脚下绽放。这尊无面的守护者像是已经在此驻足了千年，静静旁观着一代又一代学者经过。
t_en: Grass has quietly crept up the folds of the statue's garments, and blue flowers bloom at its feet. The faceless guardian appears to have stood here for millennia, silently observing generations of scholars as they pass by.

t_ch: 不知道它究竟是在守护树庭，还是在等待某个永远不会归来的人。
t_en: One wonders whether it guards the Grove or waits for someone who will never return.

t_ch: 你打量着眼前这座古老的祭坛，在树根的怀抱下，金色的蝴蝶破茧而出，它们在半空中编织出转瞬即逝的图案。
t_en: You study the ancient altar before you. Under the embrace of tree roots, golden butterflies emerge from their cocoons, weaving fleeting patterns in midair.

t_ch: 或许这就是记忆的本质——被时光包裹，却永远不会磨灭。
t_en: Perhaps this is the essence of memory — wrapped by the passage of time, but never truly erased.

t_ch: 金色的光辉从神坛底部的根系间流淌而出，像是墨涅塔的丝线编织出的记忆之网。
t_en: Golden light flows out from the roots beneath the altar, like threads spun by Mnestia, weaving a web of memories.

t_ch: 这大概就是为什么翁法罗斯的人们总说记忆是最难被掩埋的——它总会找到照进黑暗的缝隙。
t_en: This may be why the people of Amphoreus often say that memory is the hardest thing to bury — it will always find cracks through which to shine into the darkness.

t_ch: 祭坛上的纹路在黑暗中若隐若现，你依稀能辨认出这是墨涅塔的象征。
t_en: The intricate patterns on the altar faintly emerge in the darkness. You can just discern that they symbolize Mnestia.

t_ch: 巨树根系间透出温和的光芒，如同被时光包裹的旧日回响。或许这就是墨涅塔停留在此的意义——就算世界崩塌，总有被值得铭记的东西。
t_en: Gentle light filters through the giant tree's roots, like echoes of days wrapped in the shroud of time. Perhaps this is why Mnestia lingers here — to remind us that even when the world crumbles, there are always things worth remembering.

t_ch: 曾经庇护众多求知者的枝叶已经稀疏，树冠上的华光也黯淡了许多，不知圣树繁盛时会是怎样壮丽的景象。
t_en: The branches that once sheltered countless seekers of knowledge are now sparse, and the radiant brilliance of the treetop has dimmed considerably. One can only imagine how magnificent the Sacred Tree must have been in its prime.

t_ch: 理性的泰坦是否能算到，自己也会被非理性的黑暗所吞噬？你很好奇。
t_en: Could the Reason Titan have foreseen that they too would be swallowed by the irrational darkness? You wonder.

t_ch: 圣树粗壮的枝干向四周努力伸展，即使光芒被黑暗所吞噬，它也依然保持着思考的姿态，仿佛在清醒地见证着自己的消逝。
t_en: The thick branches of the sacred tree stretch outwards, persisting in their posture of contemplation, as if bearing witness, with clarity, to their own vanishing.

t_ch: 你眺望着高耸入云的圣树，叶片间闪烁着点点微光，像是天上坠落的星辰。
t_en: You gaze upon the towering sacred tree, its leaves twinkling like fallen stars.

t_ch: 不知是谁说过，知识之树总是夜深人静时开花——或许它现在就在孕育着此生最后一次的绽放。
t_en: Someone once said that the tree of knowledge blooms in the quiet of the night — perhaps it is now preparing for its final bloom.

t_ch: 通往树冠的空中连廊，由「神识之蝶」把守的要道。你想起上次途经此处时，并未有闲暇帮三月七拍摄绝景的照片，现在似乎是个补救的好机会。
t_en: The airborne pathways leading to the treetop are guarded by the Butterfly of Divine Mind. You recall how, on your last visit here, you hadn't had the time to capture this breathtaking scene for March 7th. Now seems like the perfect opportunity to make up for it.

t_ch: 拍摄「神识之蝶」。
t_en: Take a photo of the Butterfly of Divine Mind.

t_ch: 你凝视着残破的环形阶梯，上面的每一块石阶都被打磨得发亮。据说那是历代学者们在此漫步、争论与求知的痕迹。
t_en: You look upon the crumbling circular staircase, each step polished smooth. It is said that this sheen is the result of generations of scholars pacing, debating, and seeking wisdom here.

t_ch: 不知那些在此踱步的智者们，可曾想过他们的户外课堂会被藤蔓占座。
t_en: Did those wise figures, who once wandered these paths, ever imagine that their outdoor classrooms would one day be claimed by vines?

t_ch: 每个清晨，学者们都在在此列队而坐，聆听贤者们讲述智慧的奥秘。长此以往，他们在台阶上留下了坐姿的印记，这大概便是知识沉淀的模样吧。
t_en: Each morning, scholars would sit in neat rows here, listening to sages unveil the mysteries of wisdom. Over time, their seated forms left imprints on the steps — perhaps this is what the sediment of knowledge looks like.

t_ch: 「最初的学者」曾在此指出：只有在辩论的时候，我们才算是真正的聊天。自那以后，这里就成了树庭的热门景点。
t_en: "The First Scholar" once declared here: only when we debate are we truly engaging in conversation. Since then, this place has become a favored gathering spot within the Grove.

t_ch: 传说智者们总能在此间辩得想要的答案，灯台燃起的暖焰，见证过无数个灵光乍现的瞬间。
t_en: It is said that sages could always argue their way to the answers they sought here. The warm flames atop the lamps bear witness to countless moments of sudden inspiration.

t_ch: 你看见壁画上刻着巨树在阳光下伸展枝条，无数智慧的火花从树冠坠落的景象。
t_en: You notice a mural etched on the wall, depicting a giant tree spreading its branches under the sunlight, with countless sparks of wisdom cascading from the treetop.

t_ch: 这便是神悟树庭最初的由来——那时瑟希斯还在翁法罗斯为子民们无私地传授着知识，有幸得到传承者变成为了它最初的信徒。
t_en: This was the origin of the Grove of Epiphany — back when Cerces selflessly imparted knowledge to the people of Amphoreus. Those fortunate enough to receive their legacy became their earliest disciples.

t_ch: 岩壁上的壁画在长久侵蚀下已经有些斑驳，画中的每一笔都在述说着树庭的荣光：第一本传世典籍在此诞生，七位贤者在此现世……
t_en: The mural on the rock face has been weathered over time, its every stroke recounting the glory of the Grove: the creation of the first sacred text, the emergence of the seven sages...

t_ch: 如今这些璀璨的时刻只剩下褪色的回声，还有脚边几丛倔强绽放的蓝花。
t_en: Now, these brilliant moments are but faded echoes, accompanied only by a few stubbornly blooming blue flowers at your feet.

t_ch: 壁画上记载着树庭的历史：巨树的顶端绘着一道金色光芒，那是瑟希斯将智慧赐予人类的时刻。
t_en: The mural records the history of the Grove: at the top of the great tree shines a golden light — the moment when Cerces granted wisdom to humanity.

t_ch: 可现在再看这一幕，你不禁倒吸一口凉气，它更像是一个即将落幕的预言——知识之光终将被黑暗吞噬。
t_en: Yet as you gaze upon this scene now, a chill runs down your spine. It feels more like a prophecy nearing its end — the light of knowledge inevitably devoured by darkness.

t_ch: 你凝视着壁画，理性本身所化的立像伫立在画面中央，放射状的光芒自其身后迸发，像是一轮被冻结的曙光。
t_en: You fix your eyes on the mural. At its center stands a statue embodying reason itself, radiating beams of light from behind — a frozen dawn.

t_ch: 树庭的学者们说，这是他们最后一次得到瑟希斯恩赐时的景象。
t_en: Scholars of the Grove say this image depicts the last time they received Cerces' blessing.

t_ch: 光环中的身影庄严肃穆，传说这是瑟希斯在第一次降下恩赐时出现的神迹。据记载，在这幅壁画完成的那天，整个树庭能听见瑟希斯授课的声音。
t_en: The figure within the halo is solemn and majestic. Legend has it this was the divine vision of Cerces' first Miracle. According to records, on the day this mural was completed, the entire Grove could hear Cerces' voice delivering a lecture.

t_ch: 而此刻，只有风吹过附近花丛的沙沙声留下。
t_en: But now, only the rustling of nearby flowers in the wind remains.

t_ch: 没有华丽的装饰，没有繁复的图案，斑驳的岩刻壁画上只有一个简单剪影立于光芒之中。
t_en: No lavish decorations, no intricate patterns — the weathered rock mural bears only a plain silhouette bathed in light.

t_ch: 树庭学者们认为这和瑟希斯的审美一致，每个细节都在传递着它的神谕：所谓理性的真谛，大概就是在纷繁中找到本质，即使是在描绘自己的时候。
t_en: Scholars of the Grove believe this simplicity aligns with Cerces' aesthetic. Every detail conveys the prophecy: the essence of reason lies in finding simplicity within complexity, even in self-representation.

t_ch: 「学医救不了翁法罗斯」——曾有助手抛下这样的话语，离开了这座微小而明亮的庭院。庭院的主人没有责怪这位助手，她知道同一种光明无法抹平所有人的心伤。
t_en: "Medicine cannot save Amphoreus!" — a former assistant once tossed out this remark before leaving the small yet radiant courtyard. The courtyard's master did not blame the assistant. She knew that a single kind of light could not heal every wounded heart.

t_ch: 「我只不过想把自己力所能及的事做好。」或许这份谦逊，就是那位庭院主人最值得被大家夸耀的骄傲。
t_en: "I'm only trying to do what I can within my limits." Perhaps this humility is the master's most admirable pride.

t_ch: 绘制着各种奇思妙想的小黑板。粉尘勾勒出孩童对世界的华丽想象，却也封存着她被禁闭于此十数年的苦闷与孤寂。
t_en: Dusty blackboard adorned with whimsical sketches. The chalk captures a child's grand imagination of the world, though it also betrays her years of confinement and isolation.

t_ch: 若是在繁盛的城邦，学者们都将惊叹隐藏其中的创思是多么地巧妙且富有想象吧。但可惜，她从未有机会将自己的所思所想展示给世界。
t_en: In a thriving city-state, scholars would surely marvel at the ingenuity and imagination concealed within these. Sadly, she never had the opportunity to share her thoughts with the world.

t_ch: 直至现在，红发的小信使依旧奔跑在路上，怀揣着沉甸甸的过往，以及理想的轻盈未来。
t_en: Even now, the little red-haired messenger runs tirelessly, carrying the heavy weight of her past and the feather-light future of her dreams.

t_ch: 圣树底部的王座，凡世智慧的顶点——无数树庭学者为抵达知识的宝座而耗尽毕生心血，但现在，它就这样毫不设防地展现在你的面前。
t_en: At the base of the Sacred Tree lies the throne, the pinnacle of mortal wisdom. Countless scholars of the Grove spent their lives striving to reach this seat of knowledge. Yet now, it stands before you, unguarded and unclaimed.

t_ch: 耳畔仿佛传来了这样的低语：远道而来的开拓者啊，你能抵挡住登上王位的诱惑吗？
t_en: A faint whisper seems to echo in your ears: Oh, Trailblazer from afar, can you resist the temptation of ascending to the throne?

t_ch: 毫无疑问，我不能。
t_en: Undoubtedly, I cannot.

t_ch: 是啊，如此恢弘却又空荡荡的王座出现在眼前时，谁能忍住坐上去的冲动？更何况，拒绝这样的诱惑对你而言，除了错失良机外还能有什么好处？
t_en: Indeed, when such a grand yet empty throne appears before you, who could resist the urge to sit upon it? Besides, what harm could refusing this temptation possibly bring, save for missing an opportunity?

t_ch: 你决定顺应心中的欲念，但先别急，在成为王之前，你还有必须要做的事。
t_en: You decide to yield to the desire in your heart. But not so fast — before becoming king, there's something you must do first.

t_ch: 你要选择你的臣民——
t_en: You need to select your subjects —

t_ch: …普通的人类。
t_en: Ordinary humans...

t_ch: 想要更符合常识的世界，很好。在这个设定下的国家，每当有人登上王座时，扎格列斯都会为{F#她}{M#他}投掷硬币，一面是伟大，一面是疯狂。
t_en: A world governed by common sense, very well. In this setting, whenever someone ascends to the throne, Zagreus will cast a coin for {F#her}{M#him} — one side for greatness, the other for madness.

t_ch: 现在，硬币已经投下，轮到你来揭晓答案了——
t_en: Now, the coin has been tossed — it's your turn to reveal the answer.

t_ch: （查看结果）
t_en: (View Result)

t_ch: （洗洗手再来查看结果）
t_en: (Wash your hands first)

t_ch: 硬币的结果是…伟大。太好了，你是位贤明的君主，兢兢业业地为治理王国而奉献了自己的全部。没有人比你更有资格坐上这个宝座。
t_en: The result is... greatness. Wonderful! You are a wise monarch, diligently dedicating yourself to the governance of your kingdom. No one is more worthy of this throne than you.

t_ch: 嗯…坐着的体验并不算太好，很硬，屁股咯得难受，芒刺在背的感受让你有些心慌，但你相信，这些都是成为王而必须经受的考验。
t_en: Hmm... Sitting here doesn't feel that great. The hard seat pokes uncomfortably against you, and the prickling sensation makes you uneasy. But you believe these are simply the trials one must endure to become a ruler.

t_ch: 很显然，你爱上了这种感觉。
t_en: Clearly, you've fallen in love with this feeling.

t_ch: …仙女木捎来讯息：在密林的彼端，学者们的圣地。
t_en: ...The White Dryad brings word: At the end of the forest, at the sacred site of scholars...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}当上了树庭之王。
t_en: {NICKNAME} has become the Sovereign of the Grove.

t_ch: 硬币的结果是…彻底疯狂！恭喜，你是位暴虐的君主，强大而蛮横无情。尽管遭受了诸多非议与阻拦，你仍然破除所有障碍，登上了宝座。
t_en: The result is... utter madness! Congratulations! You are a tyrannical ruler — powerful, ruthless, and unyielding. Despite much criticism and resistance, you have overcome all obstacles to claim the throne.

t_ch: 你抚摸来之不易的王位，冰冷而坚实，就像是你此刻的心情。你的手指被锋利的边缘划伤，鲜血流了出来，金属纹饰染上殷红，仿佛某种不祥的诅咒。
t_en: You run your hand over the hard-won seat. It is cold and unyielding, mirroring your current mood. Your fingers are nicked by the sharp edges, drawing blood. The crimson stains the metallic patterns like a sinister curse.

t_ch: 但你不会后悔，因为你已经爱上了这种感觉。
t_en: Yet you won't regret any of this, since you've fallen in love with this feeling.

t_ch: …仙女木捎来讯息：在密林的彼端，学者们的圣地。
t_en: ...The White Dryad brings word: At the end of the forest, at the sacred site of scholars...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}当上了树庭之王。
t_en: {NICKNAME} has become the Sovereign of the Grove.

t_ch: …奇幻点的更好？
t_en: ...Maybe a tad more fantasy?

t_ch: 想要更具幻想色彩的世界，很好。你背负着巨剑与两瓶石榴汁，只身来到了决战平台，挡在你身前的是那位熟悉的盗火行者。
t_en: You want a more fantastical world. Great choice. You shoulder a giant sword and carry two bottles of pomegranate juices as you approach the final battle platform alone. Standing in your way is the familiar Flame Reaver.

t_ch: 「只懂得作战的莽夫，天谴之矛，尼卡多利；困于回忆的怯懦者，永夜之帷，欧洛尼斯；作茧自缚的大愚者，裂分之枝，瑟希斯……」
t_en: "Combat-brained brute, Nikador, the Lance of Fury. Coward shackled by memories, Oronyx, the Veil of Evernight. Great Fool trapped in the cocoon, Cerces, the Bough of Rift..."

t_ch: 「一群自以为是的泰坦，还有你，逐火而来的无名客，你们全是一丘之貉！」
t_en: "A band of self-righteous Titans. And you, the Nameless here seeking the Flame — you're all the same filth!"

t_ch: 黑衣剑士拔出造型古怪的剑，直指你的胸口。看来一场恶战在所难免——
t_en: The black-clad swordsman unsheathes a strangely shaped blade and points it at your chest. A fierce battle is inevitable...

t_ch: 后续呢？
t_en: What happens next?

t_ch: 好吧，人类没法进行两场相同的战斗，即使在你的幻想中也不行。不管如何，你击败了那位剑士，获得了最终胜利。
t_en: Well, no one can fight the same battle twice, not even in your imagination. Regardless, you defeated the swordsman and claimed ultimate victory.

t_ch: …仙女木捎来讯息：在密林的彼端，学者们的圣地.
t_en: ...The White Dryad brings word: At the end of the forest, at the sacred site of scholars...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}当上了树庭之王。
t_en: {NICKNAME} has become the Sovereign of the Grove.

t_ch: 在王的治理下，想必人们会如此形容——那是个「开拓时代」。
t_en: Under your rule, it would surely be described as an "Era of Trailblazing."

t_ch: …混乱邪恶！
t_en: ...Chaotic Evil!

t_ch: 想要更惊险刺激的世界，很好。想想你来这的旅程吧，穿过幽暗的密林，遭遇无数模样骇人的怪物的热烈欢迎，还顺势将路途中的宝物全都搜刮干净。
t_en: Craving more thrilling adventures? Perfect. Recall your journey here — passing through shadowy forests, greeted warmly by monstrous creatures, and naturally looting every treasure along the way.

t_ch: 如此一扫而空的行事作风，不由得让你想起了故事里的某位角色。于是你学着他的模样整理了头发，随后向无人的王座走去。
t_en: This pillaging style reminds you of a certain character from a story. Mimicking their manner, you slick back your hair and stride towards the unoccupied throne.

t_ch: 现在可不是休息的时候……
t_en: Now is not the time to rest...

t_ch: 可惜王座周围并没有挂着画像，你没法拿大地兽的蛋砸过去。
t_en: Too bad there aren't any portraits hanging around the throne — you can't smash the egg of a dromas against them.

t_ch: 等等，我只是想打个卡。
t_en: I just want to take a selfie.

t_ch: 好吧，如此简单而纯粹的愿望当然该得到满足。那么，就请坐上你的王位吧。
t_en: Alright, such a simple and pure wish deserves to be granted. So, go ahead and take a seat.

t_ch: 咔嚓——
t_en: Click —

t_ch: 打卡结束。好在这里并没有负责收费的管理员，你完全可以跟从自己的心意，坐到世界毁灭都没有关系——如果瑟希斯不来找你算账的话。
t_en: It's done. Thankfully, no fee-collecting administrators are present. You can follow your heart and remain seated here until the world crumbles — if Cerces doesn't come to make you pay.

t_ch: 怎么就抵挡不住了？
t_en: Why couldn't you resist?

t_ch: 你成功了！不会再有人来质疑你的意志力，你是最终的胜者！但…代价是什么呢？
t_en: You've succeeded! No one will question your resolve again. You are the ultimate victor! But... at what cost?

t_ch: 这里算得上是个小型图书馆——到处都是摊开的书籍和预言卷轴，带着涂鸦的笔记散落在地，破旧的玩具随意地摆在房间各处。
t_en: This room could be described as a miniature library — books and prophetic scrolls lie open everywhere, doodled notes are scattered across the floor, and worn toys are carelessly strewn about.

t_ch: 对外人而言，这一切都有些难以置信。谁能想到传说中的圣女，终究只是爱涂画的孩子。
t_en: To an outsider, it all seems unbelievable. Who would have thought that the legendary Holy Maiden was, in the end, just a child who loved to draw?

t_ch: 或许在那段晦暗的童年中，缇宝将大部分时光都花费在了这些石板上。
t_en: Perhaps Tribbie spent most of her time during those grim childhood years with these stone slates.

t_ch: 你试图在堆积成山的石板中找到一些轻松有趣的儿童读物，但很遗憾，即便在作为一个「真正的小孩子」时，缇宝的阅读内容依旧*稍稍*超出你的知识范围。
t_en: You try to find some lighthearted children's literature among the mountainous pile of slates, but to your dismay, even as a "true child," Tribbie's reading material was just slightly beyond your knowledge base.

t_ch: 你好，请你帮我们取来一个能被定义为「树种」的物品，它位于步行难以抵达的位置，且视线范围内有个扎格列斯之手的石台。
t_en: Greetings. Please retrieve for us an object that can be defined as a "Tree Seed." It is located in a place difficult to reach by foot, with a stone platform bearing the Hand of Zagreus within view.

t_ch: 我们，不会操控，诡计之神，厌恶。求帮忙，感谢。
t_en: We, lack control. God of Trickery, despised. Aid requested. Gratitude.

t_ch: …快走吧，这里很危险。
t_en: Quickly now, this place is dangerous.

t_ch: 树庭学者并没有那么脆弱。比起生命，研究才是更值得守护的东西。
t_en: We Grove scholars are not so fragile. To us, research is worth far more than life itself.

t_ch: 这个树种是莲食学派近几个光历年来最受内部期盼的课题成果——「种子为何是宝贵的资源」，必须把它安全带回去。
t_en: This Tree Seed represents the culmination of years of effort by the Lotophagists — a long-anticipated project titled "Why Seeds Are a Precious Resource." It must be brought back safely.

t_ch: 我们的，心血，和翁法罗斯的，未来。
t_en: Our... efforts, and Amphoreus'... future.

t_ch: 你们说话方式都怪怪的。
t_en: You all have a weird way of talking.

t_ch: 我是绳结学派的学者，致力于追求语言的精确性，你感知到的*陌生*便是来源于此，这和我的预期一致，而至于这位……
t_en: I am a scholar of Nodism, dedicated to the pursuit of linguistic precision. The unfamiliarity you sense stems precisely from this, as expected. As for this one...

t_ch: 我，赤陶学派，相信自然语言。学习山之民，成果显著。
t_en: I, an Erythrokeramist, am a believer in natural language. Studied the ways of the Mountain Dwellers. The results are evident.

t_ch: 正如你所见。别太在意，无聊的门户之争罢了。
t_en: As you can see. Pay no mind — it's just a tedious rivalry of schools.

t_ch: 好，我去去就回。
t_en: Alright, I'll be back shortly.

t_ch: 感谢。愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: Thank you. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: …怎么样，取到那个能被定义为「树种」的物品了吗？请交给我吧。
t_en: So? Did you retrieve the object defined as a "Tree Seed"? Please hand it over to me.

t_ch: 根据，面部表情，收获，肯定有。别给他，给我，给你报酬。
t_en: Judging... by facial expressions, you have. Don't give it to him. Give it to me. Reward for you.

t_ch: 所以，问题来了——你要把树种交给哪位学者呢？
t_en: Now, here's the question — who will you give the Seed to?

t_ch: 说说看，树种好处都有啥？
t_en: Tell me, what's so great about the Seed?

t_ch: …好处？做学术的事，怎么能叫好处呢？其中蕴藏着生命的奥秘，形而上的哲思，数代学者的心血，是纯粹理性的结晶！
t_en: What's so great? How could academic work be called "great"? Within it lies the mystery of life, the metaphysical essence, the painstaking efforts of generations of scholars — a crystallization of pure reason!

t_ch: 别听，都是空话。树种，种土里，亩产，一万七。解决，照明危机。
t_en: Don't listen. Empty talk. Seed... plant in soil. Yield... <unbreak>17,000</unbreak> per acre. Solve... lighting crisis.

t_ch: 我决定…
t_en: I decide...

t_ch: 给我吧。我会在结课报告里为你留下第二作者的位置，还会在致谢环节诚挚赞扬你的美德，往后，你就算是在树庭的核心期刊上发表过论文了。
t_en: Give it to me. I'll credit you as the second author in my final report and express sincere gratitude for your virtue in the acknowledgments. From then on, you'll have a publication in one of the Grove's core journals.

t_ch: 给我。我给你，利衡币，学位，换不来报酬。
t_en: Give it to me. I offer you... Balance Coins. Degree. Cannot trade... for unpaid effort.

t_ch: 把树种交给严肃的学者。
t_en: Give the Seed to the serious scholar.

t_ch: 不错，这才是求学该有的态度。等着我的论文发表吧，你会在其中找到你的名字的。我保证！
t_en: Good choice. This is the attitude every true seeker of knowledge should have. Watch for my paper's publication — you'll find your name in it, I promise!

t_ch: 这时你才想起来，那位学者似乎并没有询问你的名字……
t_en: It's only now you realize — the scholar never asked for your name...

t_ch: 把树种交给认真的学者。
t_en: Give the Seed to the earnest scholar.

t_ch: 好。翁法罗斯，照明很差，我们，都要想办法。这些，给你花。
t_en: Good. Amphoreus... poor lighting. We... must find solutions. Here... take these flowers.

t_ch: 呃…好吧，没想到铁拳的威力竟有如此之大，直接将树种砸了个粉碎。你不知道这是否是扎格列斯的恶作剧，但无论如何，现在你可没法向那两位学者交差了。
t_en: Uh... well, you didn't expect the sheer force of those iron fists to smash the seed into fragments. You're unsure if this is one of Zagreus's tricks, but either way, there's no explaining this to the two scholars now.

t_ch: …趁他们没发现，还是赶紧离开吧。
t_en: Best leave quickly before they notice.

t_ch: 剑锋向处，誓言以血色浮现。
t_en: Where the blade points, a crimson oath is revealed.

t_ch: 预言典籍与玩具诡异地共处一室——堆满灰尘的布偶旁是半掩的预言卷轴，蒲团上散落的糖果纸边上是密密麻麻的笔记。
t_en: This is a strange coexistence of prophetic texts and toys — dust-covered cloth dolls lie next to half-unfurled scrolls, while scattered candy wrappers sit beside densely scribbled notes on a meditation mat.

t_ch: 空旷的房间里，沉默的蒲团是唯一的见证者，默默守护着被困于此的纯真孩童。
t_en: In the empty room, the silent meditation mat stands as the sole witness, quietly guarding the innocent child confined here.

t_ch: 你很难想象，一个孩子如何能在如此有限的空间内熬过漫长的时光。反正你肯定做不到——即便是在黄金至尊豪华的车厢里也不行！
t_en: It's hard to imagine how a child could endure such a long time in such a limited space. You know for certain you couldn't — not even in the most opulent, gold-laden carriages of luxury!

t_ch: 通体银白的雕像笔直耸立，一眼望去总让人觉得它头顶的兜帽下藏着三张面孔。 果然是命运三泰坦的作风——连雕像都要一座顶三座的份量。
t_en: A silver-white statue stands tall and upright, its hooded head giving the illusion of hiding three faces. Truly fitting for the Three Titans of Fate — nothing less than triple the presence, even in their statues.

t_ch: 这尊雕像仿佛将「命运」二字具象化了——它的形态既像一把钥匙，又似一扇门，更像一架天平。难怪命运三泰坦的信徒们对它青睐有加。
t_en: The statue seems to embody the concept of "destiny" itself — its form resembling a key, a door, and a scale all at once. Small wonder the followers of the Three Titans of Fate hold it in such high regard.

t_ch: 高耸入顶的雕像线条流畅而富有韵律，三道火焰般的纹路从底座一直蜿蜒至顶，在兜帽处诡异地交汇。
t_en: Towering toward the ceiling, the statue's flowing lines are both rhythmic and elegant. Three flame-like patterns spiral from the base to the top, converging eerily at the hood.

t_ch: 两侧的火盆不知燃烧了多久，却始终保持着相同的高度。 真不知是雕像在守护火焰，还是火焰在守护着某个古老的预言。
t_en: Flanking it are braziers that burn with unwavering flames, their height unchanged no matter how long they've been alight. It's unclear whether the statue protects the flames or if the flames guard some ancient prophecy.

t_ch: 远处模糊的人影静静伫立在悬崖边缘。永恒之城的建筑轮廓隐没在薄雾中，宛如一座虚幻的天空之城。
t_en: In the distance, a faint silhouette stands quietly at the edge of a cliff. The outline of the Eternal City emerges faintly from the mist, like a dreamlike castle in the sky.

t_ch: 有时候真难分清，到底是刻法勒在守护这座城市，还是这座城市在守护着最后的光明。
t_en: At times, it's hard to tell — does Kephale guard the city, or does the city protect the last remnants of light?

t_ch: 石桥如同巨人的手臂横亘在深谷之上，将破碎的山体重新连结。远处的行人仿佛凝固在画中，与生机盎然的藤蔓一同点缀着这幅神话图景。
t_en: A stone bridge stretches across the abyssal chasm like a giant's arm, reconnecting the shattered mountain. In the distance, passersby seem frozen in time, adorning the mythic landscape alongside vibrant vines.

t_ch: 剑锋向处，誓言以血色浮现。
t_en: Where the blade points, a crimson oath is revealed.

t_ch: 翻越了万千门径的勇敢女孩，最终在此处停下脚步。
t_en: The brave girl who crossed the myriad passages eventually stopped here.

t_ch: 但愿所有在命运的漫漫长路中求索的迷途者，都能在那鲜花芬芳的西风尽头重聚。
t_en: May all who have lost their way on the long path of fate meet again at the end of the west wind, where flowers bloom in spring.

t_ch: 你也想过是否能用欧洛尼斯的力量，再听一句初见的问候：「*我们*是缇~安！」
t_en: You've also thought about if you could lend the power from Oronyx, for you want to hear the greeting from your first encounter: "*We* are Trianne~!"

t_ch: 可惜做不到。想将往渡冥界的灵魂复现到现世…还是太难了。
t_en: It was a shame that you couldn't. To bring the soul that crossed the nether realm back to the world of living... It's too difficult.

t_ch: 妈妈！你看这个！我失败了好多次，终于成功啦！
t_en: Momma! Look at this! It kept going wrong but I finally made one that works!

t_ch: 这是…八音盒吗？缇里西庇俄丝，你的手可真巧。以后给你讲故事的时候，就有好听的音乐伴奏啦。
t_en: Is this... a music box? Tribios, you're so talented! Now whenever I'm telling you a story, we can have beautiful music playing in the background.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，哪里有妈妈的手巧呀！妈妈可是能把整块的红石头，一点点磨成晶莹剔透的…艺术品呢！
t_en: Hehe, I'm still not as talented as you, mommy! You can take a whole block of red stone and slowly carve it into a crystal clear... work of art!

t_ch: 下一次，我要做一副…小手套！这样妈妈打磨红石头的时候，就不会伤到手啦！
t_en: Next time I want to make... a pair of gloves! That way, you won't hurt your hands next time you're polishing rocks, mommy!

t_ch: 傻孩子，要是妈妈真的怕疼，为什么不向神殿的叔叔阿姨们要手套呢？
t_en: Silly child. If I was scared of hurting my hands, why would I not just ask the old folks at the temple for a pair of gloves?

t_ch: 你呀，就别操心啦。多吃饭、多识字、多读读翁法罗斯的故事，看到你茁壮成长，妈妈比什么都高兴。
t_en: Honestly, you mustn't fuss over me. Just make sure you eat plenty, practice your reading and read lots of stories of Amphoreus. Nothing makes mommy happier than seeing you grow and thrive.

t_ch: 我有好好吃饭的！也有好好识字！至于读故事…嘿嘿，好多东西还看不懂嘛……
t_en: I have eaten plenty! And I've practiced my reading lots! But, the stories... Hehe, there are still lots that I can't read yet...

t_ch: 哎呀，原来如此，你做这个小八音盒，就是想偷懒，让妈妈给你讲更多故事，对不对呀？
t_en: Ah, so that's what you're up to. You were making this music box to slack off so mommy would read you more stories, right?

t_ch: 小机灵鬼——看来，下次要少给你讲些扎格列斯的故事了。
t_en: You sneaky thing... Looks like I won't be reading any more stories of Zagreus for a while!

t_ch: 那么，今天读什么好呢？金苹果选美…弥卡斯王…歌耳戈猎狮…这些你都听了好多遍了吧？
t_en: Well then, what story should we read today? The golden apple beauty contest... The story of King Mikas... Gorgo and the Lion... You've heard them all lots of times, haven't you?

t_ch: 没关系！只要是妈妈讲的故事，怎么都听不厌的！
t_en: I don't mind! I'll never get sick of them as long as you're reading them to me, mommy!

t_ch: 圣女大人，对于布利萨城的未来…命运泰坦们给予了怎样的神谕？
t_en: Great Maiden, what prophecy have the Fate Titans shown you... regarding the fate of Bulsa City?

t_ch: 神谕如此诉说……
t_en: The prophecy was spoken thusly...

t_ch: ——未曾得那万目之一目相视，却甘与一剑之万锋相敌。
t_en: ...Though I have never met the gaze of ten thousand eyes, I will fight against the blades of ten thousand enemies.

t_ch: ——微风将受狂啸淹没，愚钝的王纾尽千金，只为自己买下亡国的漂泊。
t_en: ...The gentle breeze shall be devoured by the roaring waves. The foolish king spends all his riches, only to purchase a life as a drifter after his nation is conquered.

t_ch: …没了么？
t_en: ...Is that all?

t_ch: 泰坦们只说了这么多。
t_en: That is all the Titans said.

t_ch: 开什么玩笑…布利萨王远道而来，只为带回拯救家乡的神谕…你难道要他失魂落魄地离开么？
t_en: What kind of joke is that... The King of Bulsa has traveled a long way for a prophecy on how to save his hometown... Are you saying you want to send him off with no hope?

t_ch: 呵…辅祭大人，你只是怕布利萨王绝望之下收回进奉的大笔钱财吧？
t_en: Hah... Sir Acolyte, you're just concerned that the King of Bulsa will take back the large amount of money he paid in his desperation, aren't you?

t_ch: 圣女，注意你的言辞…这里是供奉雅努斯火种的圣地，不是扎格列斯的贼窝！
t_en: Holy Maiden, watch your tongue... This is the holy land of the Coreflame of Janus, not Zagreus' den of thieves!

t_ch: 您不必如此失态。如今我早已失去了圣女的所谓传谕权，按我们的约定…我只负责聆听泰坦之声。
t_en: There is no need for such outrage. I have long since lost the so-called prophetic rights of the Holy Maiden. According to our agreement... I am solely responsible for listening to the word of the Titans.

t_ch: 你们如何摆弄神谕，我早已无从过问。
t_en: I have never overly pried into how it is that you manipulate these prophecies.
...Hmph.

t_ch: …只是，看在我们数十年交情的份上，还是要提醒您一句。
t_en: ...However, on the basis of our decades-long friendship, I must give you a word of warning.

t_ch: 切莫假托神谕之名，给人以虚假的希望。否则…命运定会将那城邦覆灭的罪责，归于你的头上。
t_en: Do not give people false hope under the guise of prophecy. Otherwise... fate will place the responsibility for that city's destruction on your head.

t_ch: 若不想在横渡冥河时被万千哀魂撕为碎片…还请审慎行事。
t_en: If you do not wish to be torn to shreds by the countless mourning dead when you cross the River of Souls... I would implore you to act with caution.
